rulings.Case Title: JACK MAMIYE et al VS. MOTION MEDIA NETWORK, INC. A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Roark O'Neill'S Motion For Protective Order And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 18, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 3, Defendant Roark O'Neill'S Motion For Protective Order And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =302/JPT

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, October 18, 2012, Line 4, DEFENDANT ALFRED WONG'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-12-517449 WITH CASE CGC-11-512046. Granted. 2329-2331-2333 Pacific Homeowners Association v. A & D Construction, Inc. (CGC-11-512046) and Brill v. Wong (CGC-12-517449) are consolidated for all purposes. rulings.Case Title: DONNA LANE VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, October 18, 2012, Line 8, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant's motion for summary judgment is granted. Plaintiff cannot establish the sexual orientation harassment cause of action because there is no evidence of severe and pervasive conduct that altered plaintiff's working conditions and created an abusive environment. As to the second through sixth causes of action, Defendant has demonstrated legitimate reasons for its conduct and plaintiff does not present substantial responsive evidence of pretext. Plaintiff never gave formal notice of a mental disability and Defendant attempted to engage in the interactive process by providing plaintiff with an ADA accommodation form. The Due Process cause of action fails because placing plaintiff on compulsory medical leave was reasonable under the circumstances and plaintiff failed to employ the Civil Service Rules' appeal process. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: NICK C. GRAY et al VS. MARGARET L. NARRON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Application For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment And Right To Attach Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, October 18, 2012, Line 20, Plaintiff'S Application For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment And Right To Attach Order. Granted. A writ of attachment for $51,145.52 is issued. Plaintiffs have satisfied their burden of showing a commercial contractual claim, the probable validity of that claim, and an ascertainable sum that is owed. Even though plaintiffs resided at the premises, plaintiffs properly characterize the parties' contractual relationship as arising from a commercial rehabilitation and management contract. Plaintiffs shall post a $10,000 bond.
